channel.title: Data & Analytics on
channel.description: Recent content in Data & Analytics on

item.title: Building & Managing Your Marketing Database in a Cookie-Less World
item.description: Your database is not glamorous. Nobody wins a standing ovation for good contact hygiene. But when it is a mess, the whole revenue engine starts coughing up hairballs. In a cookie-less world, the old lazy habits get expensive fast. If you want a database that actually helps marketing and sales instead of quietly sabotaging them, you need better inputs, better discipline, and a little less obsession with collecting every possible field like you are building a hostage dossier.

item.title: Exploring Intent Data's Significance in Business Strategy
item.description: Exploring the Significance of Intent Data in Business Strategy Intent data gets sold like a crystal ball. It is not. What it is can still be useful: a set of signals that helps you understand which accounts are showing interest, what topics they care about, and where sales and marketing should probably focus before they waste energy on the wrong people. The catch is that not all intent data means the same thing.
